item.title: Second edition is here!

item.title: Book Review: Release It!
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
I've been meanôÙing to read this book for a long time, and fiôÙnalôÙly got the chance and finôÙished it! It's been a fanôÙtasôÙtic jourôÙney throughôÙout the main imôÙporôÙtant topôÙics of modôÙern softôÙware arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 1 minutos de lectura)

item.title: A View on FOSDEM 2020
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Another year, another FOSDEM edition. As always, since this conference grew so big (fact: if you tried to watch all videos in a row, it'll take you about 9 weeks!), chances are, every review you read from the conference will contain something different, and therefore, complementary.

This is what I was able to exôÙpeôÙriôÙence. Let's take a look.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 3 minutos de lectura)

item.title: 2019 in Review
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
AnôÙothôÙer year went by, and alôÙthough I haven't blogged that much this year, quite a few things did hapôÙpen. Let's take a quick reôÙview of them.

As you know, I atôÙtendôÙed FOSôÙDEM 2019, back in FebruôÙary, and it was an exôÙhilôÙaôÙratôÙing evenôÙt.

I alôÙso took the opôÙporôÙtuôÙniôÙty to dive more inôÙto funcôÙtionôÙal proôÙgramôÙming with Scala, by comôÙpletôÙing the onôÙline course in CoursôÙera (which I highôÙly recôÙomôÙmend).

On the open source fronôÙt, I startôÙed with some humôÙble conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtions to the grôÙpc proôÙjecôÙt, with the goal to add asynôÙchroôÙnous supôÙport for the Python imôÙpleôÙmenôÙtaôÙtion.

I alôÙso read many inôÙterôÙestôÙing bookôÙs. Among those about softôÙware enôÙgiôÙneerôÙing, I would alôÙso like to recôÙomôÙmend SoftôÙware CreôÙativôÙiôÙty 2.0 by Robert Glass.

What to look forward to for the 2020? I would like to repeat the FOSDEM experience, and keep on contributing to open source projects, as much as possible. There's also the possibility (however I can't promise anything), that I might start a YouTube channel, with content similar to the one on this blog. But as always there will be more that I can't anticipate; the best is usually the unexpected!

HapôÙpy 2020 to evôÙeryôÙbody!

FOSDEM19 it's just over, and here are my main highlights!

item.title: 2018 in review
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
It was a great year, let's take a look at the main highôÙlightôÙs.

EarôÙly in JanôÙuary, I preôÙsentôÙed a talk at a loôÙcal meeôÙtup, which had a good reôÙcepôÙtion amongst the auôÙdiôÙence. The fist part of the year was a great peôÙriôÙod for learnôÙing more about disôÙtribôÙuted sysôÙtemôÙs, event sourcing, and evenôÙt-ôÙdrivôÙen arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures. The peak of which was atôÙtendôÙing the KafôÙka sumôÙmit in LonôÙdon.

By late AuôÙgust/earôÙly SepôÙtemôÙber I finôÙished my book Clean code in Python which was a quite chalôÙlengôÙing exôÙpeôÙriôÙence.

It was a great pleaôÙsure to atôÙtend the Python San SeôÙbastiûÀn conôÙferôÙence. I've heard about this conôÙferôÙence in a lightôÙning talk in EuôÙroPyôÙthon, and I was cuôÙriôÙous about it. It was good to meet a group of friendôÙly new peoôÙple, and havôÙing the opôÙporôÙtuôÙniôÙty to present there (an enôÙhanced verôÙsion of the talk preôÙsentôÙed at the meetôÙup in JanôÙuary). BeôÙsides, the city is amazôÙing, so it's cerôÙtainôÙly someôÙthing to reôÙpeat!

BeôÙsides some imôÙproveôÙments in my conôÙfigôÙuôÙraôÙtion files (vim and dot files), and to some of my othôÙer pubôÙlic reposôÙiôÙtoôÙries, there wasôÙn't that much room for open source conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtionôÙs. On the othôÙer hand, there was a good deal of learnôÙing: I've read some fanôÙtasôÙtic books about softôÙware enôÙgiôÙneerôÙing (such as Facts and falôÙlaôÙcies of softôÙware enôÙgiôÙneerôÙing, ObôÙjecôÙt-oriôÙentôÙed softôÙware conôÙstrucôÙtion, and more), which unôÙforôÙtuôÙnateôÙly I wasôÙn't able to covôÙer in blog posts (but maybe in the fuôÙture I will), and I sucôÙcessôÙfulôÙly comôÙpletôÙed the maôÙchine learnôÙing course ofôÙfered by CoursôÙera, which was amazôÙing. Again, maybe in a fuôÙture post I will be able to propôÙerôÙly reôÙview it, but for now I can just tell you that is highôÙly recôÙomôÙmendôÙable, if you're lookôÙing forôÙward to learn a ton and at the same time have fun solvôÙing proôÙgramôÙming asôÙsignôÙments.

All in alôÙl, it was a year with a lot of posôÙiôÙtive notes. LookôÙing forôÙward to an amazôÙing 2019 for evôÙeryôÙone!

item.title: Subtleties of Python
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
In our proôÙfesôÙsion, atôÙtenôÙtion to deôÙtail is of utôÙmost imôÙporôÙtance. Any good softôÙware enôÙgiôÙneer unôÙderôÙstands that deôÙtails matôÙter a lot, as they make the difôÙferôÙence beôÙtween a workôÙing unit or a disôÙasôÙter 1.

This is why clean code is not just about forôÙmatôÙting or arôÙrangôÙing the code. It's neiôÙther a foible. It is inôÙstead, payôÙing atôÙtenôÙtion exôÙactôÙly to those deôÙtails that will make a big difôÙferôÙence in proôÙducôÙtion.

Let's see some exôÙamôÙples of this in Python.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 4 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Notes on the Kafka Summit London 2018
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
These are some notes and takeôÙaways on the reôÙcentôÙly celôÙeôÙbratôÙed KafôÙka SumôÙmit 2018 in LonôÙdon.

The conôÙferôÙence was orôÙgaôÙnized in three parôÙalôÙlel tracks for sesôÙsions that were covôÙerôÙing stream proôÙcessôÙing, pipeliôÙnes, and inôÙterôÙnalôÙs. To get a good exôÙpeôÙriôÙence, I atôÙtendôÙed talks of the three typeôÙs, but with a litôÙtle prefôÙerôÙence toôÙwards inôÙterôÙnals and streamôÙs.

It was a two-ôÙday conôÙferôÙence with lots of valuôÙable techôÙniôÙcal conôÙtent, aweôÙsome talkôÙs, speakôÙerôÙs, and a lot more. Here are the highôÙlightôÙs.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 1 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Exploring Generators and Coroutines
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Let's reôÙvisôÙit the idea of genôÙerôÙaôÙtors in Python, in orôÙder to unôÙderôÙstand how the supôÙport for corouôÙtines was achieved in latôÙest verôÙsions of Python (3.6, at the time of this writôÙing).

By reôÙviewôÙing the mileôÙstones on genôÙerôÙaôÙtors, chronoôÙlogôÙiôÙcalôÙly, we can get a betôÙter idea of the evoôÙluôÙtion that lead to asynôÙchroôÙnous proôÙgramôÙming in Python.

We will reôÙview the main changes in Python that reôÙlate to genôÙerôÙaôÙtors and asynôÙchroôÙnous proôÙgramôÙming, startôÙing with PEP-255 (SimôÙple GenôÙerôÙaôÙtors), PEP-342 (CorouôÙtines via EnôÙhanced GenôÙerôÙaôÙtors), PEP-380 (SynôÙtax for delôÙeôÙgatôÙing to a SubôÙ-ôÙGenôÙerôÙaôÙtor), and finôÙishôÙing with PEP-525 (AsynôÙchronous GenôÙerôÙaôÙtors).

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 16 minutos de lectura)
